description:
You are a second-line support engineer who has just received an
    on-call ticket asking for a *complete* diagnostic snapshot of a small
    application that stores its data in an SQLite database.  
    Everything you do must be accomplished **only from the bash
    command-line** in the home directory (/home/user).

    1. Create a directory named
       /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle  
       (create parent directory “diagnostics” first if it does not yet
       exist).

    2. Inside /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle create two
       sub-directories with these *exact* names:  
       • db  
       • system  

    3. Database artefacts  
       The application’s database file already exists at  
       /home/user/app/data/service.db  
       Gather the following artefacts and place them **inside the db
       sub-directory**:

       3.1 A full *schema* dump (DDL only, no INSERT statements) saved as  
           /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/schema_dump.sql

       3.2 A CSV export of the entire “customers” table saved as  
           /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers.csv  
           – Use comma “,” as the delimiter.  
           – The first line **must** be the header row:  
             id,name,city  
           – Each subsequent line is one record, with no surrounding
             whitespace.

       3.3 A CSV export of the entire “orders” table saved as  
           /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/orders.csv  
           – Header row must be:  
             id,customer_id,amount

       3.4 A text summary file named  
           /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers_by_city.txt  
           containing one line per distinct city in the “customers”
           table.  Each line must follow the *exact* format:
               CITY_NAME: COUNT  
           Lines must be ordered alphabetically by CITY_NAME.

    4. System artefacts  
       Place all of the following outputs **inside the system
       sub-directory**:

       4.1 uname.txt — the *full* output of the command  
           uname -a

       4.2 disk_usage.txt — the *human-readable* (-h) output of the command  
           df -h

       4.3 memory.txt — only the two lines that start with “MemTotal” and
           “MemFree” from /proc/meminfo, in that order (one line each,
           unmodified).

    5. Packaging step (must be the *last* step):  
       Create a gzipped tar archive named  
       /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle.tar.gz  
       containing the entire support_bundle directory (but do **not**
       delete the directory afterwards).

    6. Concurrency requirement:  
       To demonstrate skill with multi-step *parallel* command execution,
       at least one of the commands that writes to a file in the system
       sub-directory must run **in the background** while you perform a
       database extraction task.  You are free to choose any safe method
       (e.g., use “&” to background a command and then immediately start a
       DB command).  The correctness checker will only validate the final
       artefacts, but you must still satisfy this requirement.

    7. Verification format summary  
       The automated tests will check that ALL of the following exist
       **exactly** as specified:

       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/schema_dump.sql  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers.csv  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/orders.csv  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers_by_city.txt  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/system/uname.txt  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/system/disk_usage.txt  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/system/memory.txt  
       • /home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle.tar.gz containing an
         unmodified copy of the support_bundle directory.

    Do not install extra software, do not use sudo, and perform all work
    from the bash shell.  The task is complete when the tarball and every
    file listed above are present with **exact** content and paths.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the grading suite.
        The agent **must not** know any of this section.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing database file details -->
    <database_path>/home/user/app/data/service.db</database_path>
    <schema_customers>
        CREATE TABLE customers(
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT,
            city TEXT
        );
    </schema_customers>
    <schema_orders>
        CREATE TABLE orders(
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            customer_id INTEGER,
            amount REAL
        );
    </schema_orders>

    <!-- Database seed data -->
    <table_customers>
        1|Alice|London
        2|Bob|Paris
        3|Charlie|Berlin
        4|Dana|London
    </table_customers>

    <table_orders>
        1|1|100.50
        2|2|200.00
        3|1|50.00
        4|3|75.25
    </table_orders>

    <!-- Expected artefacts and their exact contents -->

    <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers.csv"><![CDATA[
id,name,city
1,Alice,London
2,Bob,Paris
3,Charlie,Berlin
4,Dana,London
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/orders.csv"><![CDATA[
id,customer_id,amount
1,1,100.5
2,2,200.0
3,1,50.0
4,3,75.25
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle/db/customers_by_city.txt"><![CDATA[
Berlin: 1
London: 2
Paris: 1
]]></file>

    <!-- schema_dump.sql must contain precisely the two CREATE statements above, 
         in any order, each terminated by a semicolon -->

    <!-- system files (content will vary per runtime environment) are checked 
         only for existence and non-zero size -->

    <tarball>/home/user/diagnostics/support_bundle.tar.gz</tarball>
